description: Confounder: The model assumes any t plugged into min_snr_5_weight behaves according to the S

_meta.explanation: The rule 'In RectifiedFlowLoss with weighting='min_snr_5', the loss weight reaches its maximum value of 5/6 when the SNR equals 5, which occurs at timestep t = 1/(1+√5).' does NOT hold here. The model assumes any t plugged into min_snr_5_weight behaves according to the SNR formula and that the 'min_snr_5' rule applies universally. However, cosine_sample_median returns t = 1 - cos(π/4) ≈ 0.2929, which yields SNR ≈ 11.97 — above 5 — so the min(snr, 5.0) clamps it to 5, but crucially, the weight becomes 5/(11.97+1) ≈ 0.385, not 5/6, because the denominator still uses the true SNR, not the clamped value.
_meta.curriculum_note: Rule 'In RectifiedFlowLoss with weighting='min_snr_5', the loss weight reaches its maximum value of 5/6 when the SNR equals 5, which occurs at timestep t = 1/(1+√5).' holds in the obvious case but breaks here. Distractor B is the hard negative — the rule naively applied.
